title: Delhi Start: Old Delhi + Food Trail
excerpt: Begin with Jama Masjid, Chandni Chowk, and practical city navigation setup.
content:
Check in around Connaught Place or Karol Bagh for central access. Start with Old Delhi landmarks and keep one focused food lane stop instead of scattered hopping.

Use metro plus short auto hops to avoid parking stress. Keep hydration and light cotton layers for full-day movement.

Evening plan: India Gate drive and early rest for a monument-heavy Day 2.

title: New Delhi Icons + Museums
excerpt: Cover Qutub, Humayun, and curated museum slots with efficient routing.
content:
Start from Qutub Minar side, then move toward Humayun's Tomb and nearby heritage cluster. Pre-book tickets where possible to cut queue time.

Lunch around Khan Market or Lodhi area keeps route compact. Add one museum block only if energy remains.

Pack by night for early Agra transfer tomorrow.

title: Delhi to Agra + Sunset Taj View
excerpt: Transfer smartly and save your first Taj experience for softer evening light.
content:
Use expressway transfer and target Agra arrival by early afternoon. Check in, refresh, and head to Mehtab Bagh or sunset viewpoint side.

Keep your main Taj visit for early next morning when crowd and heat are lower.

Try Mughlai dinner but keep it light before a sunrise schedule.

title: Taj Mahal Sunrise + Agra Fort
excerpt: Do Taj in the golden window, then complete Agra Fort and local craft lane.
content:
Enter Taj Mahal at opening time for better photos and lower queue pressure. Keep at least 2 to 3 hours for the complex.

After breakfast, visit Agra Fort and optionally one marble inlay workshop. Avoid over-shopping stops that consume your Jaipur transfer buffer.

Leave for Jaipur by evening or next early morning based on comfort.

title: Agra to Jaipur via Fatehpur Sikri
excerpt: Break the long transfer with one historical stop and arrive Jaipur by evening.
content:
Leave Agra after breakfast and halt at Fatehpur Sikri for a focused 60-90 minute visit. Keep local guide usage optional but time-boxed.

Continue to Jaipur and check in near MI Road/Bani Park for practical city access.

Use evening for relaxed market walk and local Rajasthani dinner.

title: Jaipur Fort Day: Amber + Jaigarh + Nahargarh
excerpt: Stack Jaipur fort circuit by elevation and finish with sunset panorama.
content:
Start with Amber Fort before crowd surge, then move to Jaigarh and Nahargarh based on energy levels. Use one cab/day vehicle for efficiency.

Lunch break in city core, then continue to Hawa Mahal and City Palace side if time allows.

Evening viewpoint at Nahargarh offers one of the best city-light scenes.

title: Pink City Markets + Departure Wrap-Up
excerpt: Close with bazaar shopping, food trail, and clean departure window planning.
content:
Spend this day in Johari and Bapu Bazaar with predefined shopping goals to avoid time sink. Add one curated cafe or thali stop for closure.

Keep departure transfer margin of at least 3 hours for airport/train movement.

Typical budget for this 7-day route: INR 22,000 to 55,000 per person based on transport style and stay tier.
